texto: You know what’s so funny and sad about us human beings? . . . We are constantly torn between the all-consuming desire to be loved and the terrifying fear of being known. Deep inside we don’t believe the two things can exist together, that if anyone really knew us, they would surely never love us, so we spend our whole lives concocting this wonderful, plastic shell that we fight like madmen to keep pristine. But eventually the plastic cracks and what is inside is a raw, quivering mass of imperfect humanity that has always been lovely and precious enough for God Himself to love.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Earlene Fowler: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “You know what’s so funny and sad about us human beings? . . . We are constantly torn between the …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, intimacy, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e love.
